Rule: no @[simp] attributes in the operational path
Scope: Global operational Lean code path
Enforcement: Rejected in implementation manifest, code review, and closeout unless the code is explicitly non-operational and the exception is recorded.

Rule: no noncomputable in the operational path
Scope: Global operational Lean code path
Enforcement: Operational definitions must remain computable; mirror/non-operational layers must be marked and isolated.

Rule: no classical helper unless isolated in allowed mirror/non-operational layer
Scope: Global operational/mirror boundary
Enforcement: Any classical use must be outside the operational path and documented as mirror/non-operational.

Rule: no Matrix.inv or external inverse oracle in operational derivations
Scope: Global inverse/elimination derivations
Enforcement: Use explicit proof-carrying inverse candidates and two-sided inverse proofs; external inverse APIs are forbidden as operational sources.

Rule: no free formula without proof-carrying entry structure
Scope: Global formula/code-object introduction
Enforcement: Every formula exposed to consumers must be backed by a source object, entry structure, definition, proof target, and verification/dossier record.

Rule: no consumer-side patch when the missing structure belongs earlier
Scope: Global dependency/backjump discipline
Enforcement: Missing structure must be introduced at its natural source layer; consumer/gate patches are blocked.

Rule: no premature obstruction before a serious positive construction attempt
Scope: Global positive-path discipline
Enforcement: Obstructions require a recorded positive attempt and a localized mathematical failure reason.

Rule: no phase-semantic public API names
Scope: Global public API and module-surface naming
Enforcement: SM/AR/phase-semantic names may remain legacy/internal but cannot be public-final neutral API.

Rule: no fragile rewrite chain when a structural constructor/projection proof is available
Scope: Global proof-tactic strategy
Enforcement: Prefer constructors, projections, and explicit structural lemmas; fragile rewrite chains must be justified as local and harmless.

Rule: do not delete legacy status enums during quarantine
Scope: Global legacy quarantine workflow
Enforcement: Legacy status enums may be moved/quarantined/wrapped, not deleted during the quarantine phase.

Rule: do not let Phase 3+ import Legacy.StatusGates
Scope: Global import firewall for post-neutral phases
Enforcement: Phase 3+ visibility checks must reject Legacy.StatusGates imports.

Rule: do not treat old TeX/PDF files as maintained roadmap sources
Scope: Global planning/source-of-truth policy
Enforcement: YAML master is authoritative; old TeX/PDF files are historical/generated views only.

Rule: no string-like or physical analogy as CNNA-generative input
Scope: All interpretive, arithmetic, Schur/DtN, and handoff phases.
Enforcement: Analogies may be comparison/diagnostic notes only; any formula, object, spectrum, or handoff must have a derived code/proof route.

Rule: no horizontal/vertical Schur-duality or string/subsystem analogy as input to earlier generators, Schur, arithmetic, or CD definitions
Scope: all Pillar-A bridge, Schur, arithmetic, CD, and generator phases
Enforcement: the duality may only be a later theorem/diagnostic target after S05/O05/TM0/CD7/BR06/BR07 are closed; earlier phases may export data to it but never consume its conclusion

Rule: no heuristic angle/lattice construction as generator or branching definition
Scope: Global generator, substrate, lattice-test, and arithmetic comparison workflows
Enforcement: Any Eisenstein/Gauss spiral or angle profile must be a consumer-only test/comparison target. The code-defined branching API is authoritative; angle drawings, CM targets, and j values may not define b, carriers, parents, or generator semantics.

Rule: no special-case-only test matrix as evidence for a general computable path
Scope: All computation, arithmetic, lattice, generator, smoke-test, and refactor-regression workflows
Enforcement: Eisenstein/Gauss or other sentinel rows may be included only as special comparison rows. Generic finite rows over the code-defined API must exist, or the phase remains unclosed/blocked.

Rule: no Heegner/class-number-one membership as closed noOuter evidence
Scope: All number-theory, CM, lattice, regime, test-matrix, AQFT/unitarity, and comparison workflows
Enforcement: A row with h(D)=1, a Heegner label, or an integer j target remains number-theoretic/comparison data until its noOuter/HasOuter regime is derived from Pillar-A source objects. Eisenstein/Gauss may be tested as noOuter-root sentinels; other Heegner targets default to HasOuter/complement-projection or comparison rows unless proven otherwise.

Rule: no replacement of ExecComplex by analytic Complex, Cayley-Dickson C-surface, or unproven exact quadratic scalar layer
Scope: global operational scalar path, arithmetic, lattice/CM, Cayley-Dickson bridge, and test-matrix phases
Enforcement: ExecComplex remains QxQ operational floor. O09 is required for later quadratic coordinates but only as a schema; concrete instances require GNG19/O10/BR14. CD-derived C may only be consumed after CD9/10.4.3 proves noncircular provenance. General Complex/Real stays mirror-only.

Rule: no free sqrt(D), no exact quadratic instance without proof-carrying D-source
Scope: all scalar, arithmetic, CM/lattice, number-theory, test-matrix, and Cayley-Dickson bridge phases
Enforcement: O09 may define only a parameterized schema. Any concrete exact quadratic row must consume O10/BR14 with a D sourced from O05/O06 or an equivalent operational discriminant ledger. Analytic Complex/Real, AdjoinRoot field structures, or CD C-surfaces may not supply the early D or theta.

Rule: no artificial phase bottleneck or phase-title/object mismatch
Scope: global phase DAG, object register, module manifest, and implementation scratchpads
Enforcement: Each object must be placed behind only the prerequisites used by its proof target. In particular, SP0/SP1/P00-P06/TM0-TM2 may not wait on modular forms, j, q-expansions, Hilbert class polynomials, theta or L-functions unless a specific bridge theorem explicitly requires that dependency.

Rule: no unexplained empty Linked-objects cell and no umbrella aggregation as direct object ownership
Scope: global phase table, object register, scratchpads, module manifest, generated views
Enforcement: Every phase must declare direct, scope_only, no_direct_object, or moved. Empty direct object sets require an explicit non-direct policy. Child object scope may be shown for readability but cannot change object Phase ownership.

Rule: no dynamics, physics, stochasticity, geometry, arithmetic, operator algebra, environment, or unitarity as ontic primitive beyond subsystem-being
Scope: global CNNA ontology, object register, phase DAG, scratchpads, proof dossiers, consumer bridges, external-comparison sections
Enforcement: Every such structure must cite a derived cut/regime/complement/Schur-DtN/source-object chain. If a phase needs one of these before the chain exists, it must backjump to the natural generating layer rather than postulate or patch the consumer.

Rule: no 100-percent truth declaration and no bypass of discipline-specific expert review
Scope: All CNNA documentation, generated tables/PDFs/graphs/exports, phase status claims, Lean-code interpretation, object proof dossiers, and established-theory/reference markings
Enforcement: The checked Lean code is the primary truth system, but even a green build only checks formal consistency of chosen declarations. Semantic correctness, modeling adequacy, theory attachments, and generated interpretations remain reviewable. Every relevant claim must remain open to specialized expert review; generated artifacts are secondary.

Rule: no ignored missing blue/reference-theory marking
Scope: All references to established mathematics/physics/computer-science background, local mathlib/formalization context, and large end-theory comparison horizons
Enforcement: If a claim, object, phase, module, or documentation passage touches established theory, the tool must decide whether it needs blue/reference marking. Missing markings are audit debt. Blue marks comparison/reference/review context only and never promotes established theory to a CNNA generator.

Rule: no tool-interface metaphor as formal CNNA theorem or ontic input
Scope: Documentation, database views, context slices, phase closeouts, and reflective tool architecture notes.
Enforcement: The bright/dark/interface analogy may guide review and visibility obligations only. It must not be cited as a proved CNNA statement, a mathematical generator, or a reason to bypass Lean-primary proof and discipline-specific expert review.

Rule: no semantic module consolidation without compatibility facade, natural-layer placement, and regression evidence
Scope: All Lean refactor/merge phases, module manifest, reuse ledger, import graph, declaration consumption map, object proof dossiers, and public API/firewall phases
Enforcement: A merge/refactor may proceed only after LEG36 classifies the candidate, preserves old import paths or supplies an explicit migration facade, records import/declaration/consumer deltas, and obtains local full-build evidence. Proof-stage chains must not be merged by naming similarity or line-count pressure.

Rule: no generated PDF/TeX/LaTeX build products or historical reports as active root sources
Scope: Repository root, generated full-document outputs, context-documentation outputs, release packaging, README/toolchain commands, and archive/report policy
Enforcement: The project root must remain source-oriented: README.md may stay in root; historical Markdown reports belong in archive/reports/; generated PDF, TeX, aux, log, out, toc, fls and fdb_latexmk files are disposable and must not be treated as authoritative or retained as active root files. Regenerate them from YAML/tables/scripts when needed.

Rule: do not remove package-root Lean/Lake project-control files during cleanup or packaging
Scope: Package root, release packaging, root cleanup workflow, local build workflow, README/manual commands, future CI/database packaging
Enforcement: Cleanup may remove disposable generated documentation artifacts and archive historical reports, but packaging must preserve package-root CNNA.lean, lakefile.lean, lake-manifest.json, and lean-toolchain unless an explicit future build-entry phase replaces them with a proven alternative.

Rule: no duplicate active README and no hidden license/credit surface
Scope: Package root, Repository root, generated full/context PDFs, release packaging, README/manual commands, license notice table, and future CI/database packaging
Enforcement: The maintained README.md and LICENSE must live in the package root. Subdirectories must not carry additional active README.md files unless a future phase classifies them as generated/raw-export artifacts with explicit non-authoritative status. Generated PDFs must expose the Editor and Assistant credits from metadata.

Rule: generated PDF section-pagebreak invariant
Scope: Full documentation generator, context documentation generator, review PDFs, future documentation layout profiles, and generated-secondary review packets
Enforcement: Top-level sections in generated PDFs must start on new pages automatically. The invariant is implemented in the generators, not by manually editing disposable TeX/PDF outputs. Layout changes must not change Lean code, Master-YAML truth semantics, or context-matching logic.

Rule: no white direct-Lean derivation phases and no release without derived-only object evidence
Scope: Phase table, implementation scratchpad, pre-implementation checks, object register, proof dossiers, active cursor selection, generated PDFs/TSVs, future database/status dashboards
Enforcement: If a phase_type is derivation and the phase is not green, yellow, or orange, it must be red. A red derivation phase may become yellow or orange only after a pre-implementation release check references the required already-derived Lean objects/proof dossiers. White is reserved for refactor/tool/infrastructure/documentation/comparison work that does not itself require direct Lean-code derivation release.

Rule: main phase table is the release surface; module-change manifest is appendix-only
Scope: Full documentation, context documentation, phase status release workflow, module-change/refactor audits, implementation scratchpads, README/manual use
Enforcement: Do not read the module-change manifest as a second phase table. It may only record concrete module/API/import-surface change contracts. Phase release status, dependencies and derived-only evidence belong to the main phase table, object register and proof dossiers. Static explanatory prose must stay generator-owned; table rows remain dynamic.

Rule: no new substantive Lean code before full IST intake, neutral naming/refactor, post-scan and release proof
Scope: All future Lean implementation prompts, 1.1.1.2 blue-object correction, Phase 1.x neutralization, public API/firewall phases, and later red locked derivation phases
Enforcement: Before any new substantive Lean content phase becomes yellow, the plan must reference closed LEG37-LEG41 or spawn a red blocker at the natural source layer. Refactoring/tooling may proceed only inside the pre-code readiness chain and must preserve old imports through facades until build-proven.

Rule: no database mirror row is a CNNA truth source; public access remains read-only
Scope: database/publication mirror, future web frontend, generated-secondary exports, review tooling and online visualization
Enforcement: No database mirror row may release a phase or replace Lean/YAML provenance. Public/public read-only access is SELECT-only on api.* views. No public writes. No browser admin write credential key. No DB update without snapshot_id, source hashes and import report. Every public row must be traceable to a Lean/YAML snapshot.

Rule: generated PDF typography changes must be generator-level and semantic-neutral
Scope: Full documentation generator, context documentation generator, table generator, graph-label generation, generated PDF review packets and future documentation layout profiles
Enforcement: Font-size changes must be applied in generators/source tables, not by manual PDF edits. They must not alter Lean code, YAML row semantics, context filtering, phase status logic, proof status, or public database/export semantics.

Rule: Quaternion/geometric-algebra references are target gates only
Scope: Horn 0709.2238v1, Hamilton quaternion relations, Pauli matrices, Clifford/geometric algebra, biquaternions, spacetime algebra, conformal GA, mathlib Quaternion/CliffordAlgebra APIs and future GA comparison modules.
Enforcement: These references may define comparison profiles, sign audits, scratchpad notes and expert-review targets only. They cannot generate CNNA objects, set scalar layers, choose orientations/dimensions, release phases, create handoff data or shortcut derived-only proofs.

Rule: Status/context orthogonality and Lean-facing subclass separation. Phase status and Object status encode release/proof-state only; Context class encodes type/class membership. No ToolInfrastructureObject, DatabaseMirrorObject, GeneratedArtifactObject, DocumentationObject, GovernancePolicyObject or ReferenceGateObject may be consumed as a LeanSourceObject/LeanTargetObject or as derived mathematical data because it is green, linked or documented. LeanRefactorObject, LeanHandoffContractObject and LeanGovernanceObject are Lean-facing control classes, not automatically mathematical CNNA data.
Scope: All phase, object, proof-dossier, scratchpad, generated export, DB mirror and implementation-prompt surfaces.
Enforcement: All new phase/object rows must declare Context class from the taxonomy. Generated tables render Context class beside status. Future DB schema must mirror the field. Any phase release must cite LeanSourceObject/LeanTargetObject evidence, not merely a green tool/reference/governance object.

Rule: no version-axis conflation
Scope: all metadata, release artifacts, generated exports, YAML2SQL snapshots, README/PDF titles, and Explorer provenance rows
Enforcement: lean_data_version records the Lean toolchain/API level and changes only on Lean/toolchain-version upgrade; lean_data_timestamp advances only with Lean source/data changes. planning-data version/timestamp advances with YAML phase/object/scratchpad/dossier/policy state changes; tool-infrastructure version/timestamp advances only with generator/importer/frontend/tool-code changes. Compatibility aliases may mirror the axes but may not collapse them.

Rule: no timestampless semantic version records
Scope: metadata, latest-change overview, generated exports, db_snapshots/YAML2SQL imports, Explorer nodes/edges/history, release filenames/reports and future public API provenance rows
Enforcement: Every persisted or exported semantic version reference must include the corresponding timestamp. Required pairs are lean_data_version+lean_data_timestamp, planning_data_version+planning_data_timestamp and tool_infrastructure_version+tool_infrastructure_timestamp. For the Lean axis, lean_data_version is the Lean toolchain/API level and lean_data_timestamp is the Lean source/data snapshot time. Any DB snapshot, generated report or Explorer history row that stores a version without its timestamp is invalid and must be rejected or marked incomplete before publication.

Rule: No destructive object overwrite after patch, rename, deletion or retirement.
Scope: All object registers, proof dossiers, scratchpads, generated exports, SQL mirrors and Explorer views.
Enforcement: A changed object must retain its old row/dossier as historical provenance, carry an explicit lifecycle state and point to successor/current object or terminal retirement. Phases are never marked outdated; corrections are represented by successor/patch phase links.

Rule: No cyclic current-successor lifecycle chains.
Scope: Object lifecycle pointers, Explorer graph edges, SQL mirror imports and history timeline.
Enforcement: Lifecycle successor/current-object edges must be acyclic and must terminate in a current object or explicit deleted-retired terminal row. Cycles are allowed only for separately typed non-derivation/reference edges and must never define object currency.

Rule: No untyped, unbucketed or lifecycle-less Lean declaration after source-intake or Explorer-readiness closure.
Scope: LEG37, CEX0, CEX11, generated declaration inventories, YAML2SQL mirror, Explorer declaration nodes and public API/refactor release gates.
Enforcement: Every Lean declaration must have declaration_kind, semantic_role, triviality_tier, significance/documentation policy, lifecycle_state, public_surface_status and owning object or explicit non-object bucket. Unknown/manual-review labels are blockers, not closure evidence.

Rule: No heuristic-only triviality or significance decision for Lean declarations.
Scope: All declaration classifiers, object/proof-dossier generation, Explorer filters and documentation-depth assignment.
Enforcement: Automatic labels from rfl/projection/body shape, declaration keyword, path or reference count are candidate labels only. Curated override must exist for boundary/handoff/fundamental contracts and for demoting ledger/status scaffolding.

Rule: No Explorer completeness claim from consumption classification alone.
Scope: LEG5, LEG37, CEX0, CEX11, SQL mirror imports, Explorer inspector panels and public-read documentation.
Enforcement: Consumption class records may feed CEX11 but cannot prove total declaration sorting. Closure requires all required axes plus owner bucket, documentation policy and lifecycle status.

Rule: No de-seeding without source map, derived-only gate, notation/readability binding, old-path regression and rollback evidence. Seed/Scaffold/Window modules may not lose their suffix or become current public API unless free cutoffs/states/dictionaries/observers are removed or explicitly downgraded to derived residual data, the importgraph is cycle-audited and the old path remains a regression oracle or lifecycle-retired object.

No full-derived QFT/AQFT face without complement-net modular chain. Bright-only recovery, finite boundary algebra, GNS/KMS scaffold or split-property-like evidence does not suffice for vN/Type-III/CPT/spin-statistics/Kramers claims unless ComplementLocalNet/ComplementStateNet/ComplementGNS/SeparatingProperty/TomitaTakesakiData/ModularConjugation/ComplementKMS/ComplementSpectrumCondition are derived or explicitly marked as open gates.

No fermionic, CPT, spin-statistics or Kramers semantics before graded algebra plus modular B chain plus D-causality/BW bridge. Statistics, spin, CPT and Kramers labels must not float on an ungraded or non-modular AQFT surface.

No silent primitive rest-parameter export after ParameterClosure. b, Lmax, beta and numerical regularizers must either be derived through BranchingWitness/selectors/HorizonLevel/BackreactionFixedPoint/RegularizationClosure or remain explicitly marked as scaffold/residual variables; they may not become hidden ontic inputs.

No matter or causal-geometry closure from name overlap. Time reversal alone is not Matter; GlobalStateCone or cone-like state families are not causal lightcones; established physics words may define audit gates, not automatic CNNA semantics.
No 1:1 REALOQS legacy code port into CNNA.
Scope: All future REALOQS migration, especially AQFT/PillarB.
Enforcement: Every legacy declaration/module must receive migration disposition and CNNA source-map before implementation. Direct import/copy is blocked unless a later phase proves semantic compatibility and still records lifecycle provenance.

Rule: No legacy A-internal or config-harness dependency in CNNA PillarB.
Scope: A->B handoff and AQFT migration.
Enforcement: PillarB may consume only explicit CNNA A->B handoff data. Legacy TreeOfCliques/Config/BInterfaces dependencies are migration risks to be inverted, not accepted inputs.

Rule: No legacy noncomputable/simp-rich AQFT layer in the operational path.
Scope: BoundaryMatrix, KMS/GNS/modular and finite boundary-matrix migration.
Enforcement: Noncomputable analytic legacy constructions must be isolated in allowed mirror/reference layers or rebuilt through operational proof-carrying structures. Legacy @[simp] proofs do not authorize operational simplification policy changes.

Rule: No Explorer or planning edge may render REALOQS legacy provenance as CNNA-derived provenance.
Scope: CNNA Explorer, SQL mirror, object lifecycle and legacy overlays.
Enforcement: Legacy nodes and edges require explicit LegacyCodeReference classes and reference/migration edge types. They cannot unlock green CNNA objects without a new CNNA-derived proof phase.

Rule: No derived-only closure from algebraic skeleton alone. A green/closed claim requires structural skeleton, discharged proof obligations and a genuine generated-data operational smoke path for the relevant claim.
Scope: All CNNA phases, especially arithmetic smoke tests, AQFT gates and Explorer status rendering.
Enforcement: A phase/object may not be marked green merely because the algebraic framework compiles; the scratchpad/dossier must identify the generated data path and its verification evidence.

Rule: No missing-structure consumer patch. If a downstream consumer exposes a missing structure, the downstream phase becomes blocked and the structure must be created at its earliest natural source layer, not inserted where the failure was observed.
Scope: All implementation and refactor phases.
Enforcement: The red-blocker workflow must record the missing structure, rejected patch site, selected natural origin, and proof/audit reason for that origin before the consumer can reopen.

Rule: No implementation-readiness claim without implementation-thought-experiment pressure test. A phase-check must simulate the implementation path sufficiently to expose likely hidden dependencies before yellow/orange status is assigned.
Scope: Pre-implementation phase checks and active-phase selection.
Enforcement: The pre-implementation check must distinguish a superficial dependency scan from a forced implementation scenario; missing dependencies found in the scenario become red blockers.

Rule: No linear-document sufficiency claim for CNNA planning. PDF/TeX views are generated secondary renderings and cannot serve as the primary machine-readable planning state once YAML/SQL/Explorer data exist.
Scope: Planning tool, Explorer, generated documentation and future database mirror.
Enforcement: The Master YAML remains the planning data source; PDFs and TeX files are review/render outputs. Claims of completeness must cite YAML/ledger checks, not page count or prose presence.

Rule: No free physical parameter export from the Foundation. Parameters such as L_max, branching strength, beta/regularizers, horizon-like quantities and backreaction inputs must have an internal origin, an explicit future backreaction handoff, or be marked unresolved.
Scope: Foundation, ParameterClosure, A->B/A->C/A->D/A->E handoffs and Explorer parameter displays.
Enforcement: Any parameter field without source provenance becomes red/orange depending on whether a natural upstream or backreaction phase exists. It cannot be silently exported as foundation data.

Rule: No stochastic primitive. Stochasticity/noise/randomization cannot be introduced as an ontic input; it must be derived from CNNA structure or kept as external comparison-only language.
Scope: Pillar A, OQS, spacetime/Causal-Set comparisons, AQFT/OQS bridge and simulation layers.
Enforcement: Explorer and planning rows must label stochastic language as derived, pending derivation, or external-reference only. No stochastic object can close a CNNA phase unless its source path is explicit.

Rule: No cyclic pillar co-development. Pillars B-E may be co-developed and mutually constraining, but only through explicit acyclic handoff/input edges; no pillar may consume its own output or a higher-pillar output outside the declared input-handoff channel.
Scope: Pillar B-E planning, handoffs and Explorer graph semantics.
Enforcement: Graph edges must separate derivation, handoff, backreaction, comparison and hypothesis semantics; cycles in proof-producing provenance are blocked even if visual co-development is allowed.

Rule: No unbacked repository automation. Assistant/API/CI-driven repository mutation must not target the default branch directly as the normal path; a recoverable backup tag/branch and a work branch/PR path are required before mutation.
Scope: GitHub repository operations, assistant-mediated changes, CI setup, release branches and generated planning zip synchronization.
Enforcement: Planning and implementation prompts must record backup ref, work branch/PR and CI status. Any direct-main mutation requires explicit emergency override and rollback evidence.

Rule: No public or unproven database write authority. Webhosting/MySQL, SQL/API and Explorer layers are read-only for public users and generated-secondary; production/current mirror state may change only through validated snapshot import/swap.
Scope: Phase 21 database mirror, Webhosting importer, public API, frontend, credentials and future cnna-project.net deployment.
Enforcement: Importer/admin credentials must stay outside browser/chat/repository plaintext. Public API tests must prove read-only behavior; SQL rows must carry commit/toolchain/hash provenance.

Rule: No deployment without rollback and local archive path. Automated CI/DB/Explorer updates require independent recovery paths for Git state, generated artifacts and MySQL mirror state.
Scope: GitHub releases, workflow artifacts, Webhosting/MySQL imports, Explorer public snapshots and local archival handoff.
Enforcement: A release cannot close deployment-hardening until backup tag/branch or bundle, artifact manifest, SQL dump/snapshot and restore instructions are recorded.

Rule: No silent CI phase-state drift. A red Lean CI build must not leave the attempted active phase in an implementable/active-success state and must not update production/current mirrors; it must mark the phase red, create or activate a predecessor repair phase at the natural source layer, and publish a visible read-only failure-history snapshot. A green Lean CI build must not advance the phase cursor unless required closeout evidence is present.
Scope: GitHub Actions, assistant/repository automation, phase closeout, red-build repair generation, MySQL/Webhosting deployment and Explorer history.
Enforcement: CEX15 transition manifests and CEX16 failure snapshots must record commit SHA, run ID, active phase, red/green conclusion, evidence files and resulting phase changes. Red transitions block merge and production/current import/deploy but publish failure history; green transitions require completed scratchpad/dossier/object evidence and must leave exactly one active cursor.
Change note: P1.39 timestamp-bounded delta record: CI red-path visibility plus delta-PDF output policy.

Rule: No hidden red builds. Failed Lean/scan runs that affect phase state must be visible as generated-secondary failure-history artifacts and linked to their repair predecessor or localization gate.
Scope: GitHub Actions, phase-state controller, Webhosting/MySQL history tables/views, Explorer/API build history, release notes and expert-review workflow.
Enforcement: A red CI transition is invalid unless it emits red_failure_snapshot.json or equivalent, exposes a read-only history/API row, links to the generated repair phase, preserves the last green production/current snapshot and records no-current-swap evidence. Sensitive credentials and excessive logs must not be public.
Change note: P1.39 timestamp-bounded delta record: CI red-path visibility plus delta-PDF output policy.

Rule: No default full-PDF churn for bounded planning changes. When a planning update changes only a timestamp-bounded set of rows, the user-facing PDF deliverable must be the delta slice of exactly those changed records; the full documentation PDF is optional or reserved for explicit full-release/audit checkpoints.
Scope: Plan-document generation, context-slice generation, release artifacts, Webhosting/Explorer documentation snapshots, review packets and archive packages.
Enforcement: Every changed YAML row must carry the same Change version and Change timestamp for the update set. The default PDF generator selects rows by that pair and emits a delta PDF plus matched-row exports. Full-PDF generation must be explicit, periodic, or release-gated and must not replace the delta as the primary review artifact for small changes.
Change note: P1.39 timestamp-bounded delta record: CI red-path visibility plus delta-PDF output policy.

Rule: No workflow self-sabotage or contradictory transition semantics. Workflow policies that affect phase status, CI evidence, MySQL/Explorer mirrors, backup/rollback, public visibility or documentation output must share one ordered state model and may not encode mutually incompatible exits.
Scope: All workflow_policy rows, phase-state controller CEX15, red-build visibility CEX16, GitHub automation, Webhosting/MySQL importer, Explorer/API history, documentation generation and phase closeout.
Enforcement: Before modifying workflow policy, run the workflow-coherence audit: distinguish missing evidence from red failure, require visible red history without production/current swap, require green closeout evidence before cursor advance, preserve read-only public mirrors, keep backup/rollback before deploy, and use delta PDFs for bounded updates unless a full release/audit checkpoint is explicit. Any contradiction must become a static finding and be patched at the workflow/source layer before implementation.
Change note: P1.40 workflow-coherence audit: clarified no-build vs red-build states, delta-PDF closeout, red-history API/deployment dependencies, and added anti-self-sabotage workflow guardrail.

Rule: No Explorer before cleanup-refactor-scan closure. Explorer SQL/API/UI work must not start from pre-refactor, partially renamed or partially mirrored source data.
Scope: Phases 1.1.1.1.5.1-1.1.1.1.5.5, 20.15-20.19, 21.0-21.9, YAML/SQL/API/Explorer tooling and implementation prompts.
Enforcement: Require the ordered gate: Notation.lean audit/cleanup -> neutral naming/facade plan -> compatibility-preserving refactor -> post-refactor full 1:1 Lean mirror scan -> Explorer readiness. Any attempted Explorer build before this order closes is red/blocked and must backjump to the earliest missing gate.
Change note: P1.41 repository-cleanup sequencing correction: Notation.lean audit/cleanup and neutral renaming/refactor must precede the full module/object scan; Explorer and further substantive Lean code remain blocked until the post-refactor 1:1 Lean mirror closes.

Rule: No pre-refactor full scan as final source truth. A full module/declaration/object scan performed before cleanup and neutral refactor may be used as provisional input only, never as the Explorer-current or release mirror.
Scope: Inventory scripts, CEX11 total declaration classification, LEG37/LEG40 source intake, DB/API/Explorer mirrors and release certificates.
Enforcement: Generated inventory must record whether it is pre-refactor or post-refactor. Only a post-refactor scan can close the 1:1 Lean-mirrored object-state gate or feed Explorer-current SQL/API views.
Change note: P1.41 repository-cleanup sequencing correction: Notation.lean audit/cleanup and neutral renaming/refactor must precede the full module/object scan; Explorer and further substantive Lean code remain blocked until the post-refactor 1:1 Lean mirror closes.

Rule: No unchecked Notation.lean public surface. Notation declarations, aliases and symbol wrappers may not remain public or drive scans merely by existing; they require actual consumer evidence or an explicit public-surface justification.
Scope: All CNNA/**/Notation.lean files, CoreSymbols/Legacy phase aliases, public API contracts, refactor plans, consumer whitelist, generated inventory and Explorer-readiness gates.
Enforcement: Before neutral renaming/refactor, produce a notation ledger with declaration, defining module, consumer modules, owner object/non-object bucket, public/internal/legacy status, keep/quarantine/remove/rename disposition and ambiguity flag. Unresolved notation becomes a blocker at the notation layer.
Change note: P1.41 repository-cleanup sequencing correction: Notation.lean audit/cleanup and neutral renaming/refactor must precede the full module/object scan; Explorer and further substantive Lean code remain blocked until the post-refactor 1:1 Lean mirror closes.
